definition: Three-candle pattern creating a gap (imbalance) where price moved too fast for balanced order flow.
depends_on: displacement (FVGs are CREATED BY displacement — no displacement, no meaningful FVG)
feeds_into: silver_bullet (silver bullet = FVG formed in specific time window)
consequent_encroachment (CE = 50% midpoint of FVG for precision entry)
related_to: displacement (FVG is the EVIDENCE of displacement)
premium_discount (FVG in wrong zone is less reliable)
bridge_integration: FVGs are detected and used in three distinct ways. (1) M15 detection drives core scoring: bridge/ict_pipeline.py:492 calls analysis.fvg.detect_fvgs(df, max_age_bars=50, min_quality=VERY_AGGRESSIVE) on the trigger TF. The resulting fvgs feed analysis.ict.scorer.score_ict_setup at scorer.py:301-318 which awards up to 15 pts (weight['fvg']) — 50% if any active FVG exists in the trade direction, +30% × quality bonus, +20% if current price is inside an aligned FVG. The result lands on SymbolAnalysis.fvg_score and the synergy_scorer treats fvg_score >= 9 as 'FVG present' (synergy_scorer.py:80). (2) Synergies and gates: 'OB + FVG overlap' adds +10 (synergy_scorer.py:347), 'Sweep + displacement + FVG' adds +7 (line 390), 'OTE in OB/FVG stack' adds +5 (line 359), 'Silver bullet + FVG' adds +5 (line 402), 'Sweep+Disp+FVG' = the complete ICT chain. The 'FVG in wrong premium/discount zone' gate subtracts 3 (line 524). (3) HTF FVG obstacle gate is the single most important application: bridge/ict_pipeline.py:494-501 detects H4 FVGs from CLOSED bars only (the forming H4 candle is excluded — phantom-FVG bug fixed 2026-04-23) at FVGQuality.DEFENSIVE; lines 668-691 set htf_fvg_obstacle=True if the trade direction would cross into an opposing H4 FVG (price inside, or within 0.5% of the boundary). This triggers the 'HTF FVG obstacle' gate (-5 in synergy_scorer.py:532) and is surfaced verbatim to Claude in claude_decision.py:954-957 as 'HTF FVG OBSTACLE: …' with explicit instruction to auto-downgrade or SKIP (claude_decision.py:831). (4) FVG-CE entry pricing: ict_pipeline.py:870-911 selects the highest-priority retracement FVG (FVG+OB stack > FVG-in-OTE > plain FVG), computes its consequent encroachment (50% midpoint), and stores it as fvg_entry_price + fvg_entry_zone. claude_decision.py:940-945 then injects 'OPTIMAL ENTRY: …' into the prompt instructing Claude to use the CE as a limit-order entry instead of market price; analysis_pipeline.py:593-594 enforces this on the executor side. Inverse FVGs are not detected in code today; they appear only in concept_injector.py:302 as a prompt warning when the HTF obstacle fires. Daily FVGs are detected in ict_pipeline.py:697-710 and added as advanced_factors ('inside_D1_FVG' / 'D1_FVG_obstacle') for Claude's prompt context but do not contribute to fvg_score directly.
bullish_FVG: Gap between candle 1 high and candle 3 low. Below current price.
bearish_FVG: Gap between candle 1 low and candle 3 high. Above current price.

consequent_encroachment.definition: The 50% midpoint of the FVG (CE) — the most precise entry point.
consequent_encroachment.connection: CE is the FVG's version of equilibrium (like dealing_range equilibrium). Price reaching CE and holding = FVG respected. Price closing through CE = FVG invalid.

timeframe_hierarchy: HTF FVGs (H4/D1) > LTF FVGs (M15/M5). A LTF FVG INSIDE a HTF FVG = highest probability. This nesting mirrors dealing_range nesting.

inversion_FVG.definition: FVG that was fully filled and traded through — now acts as opposite S/R.
inversion_FVG.connection: Inverted FVGs become OBSTACLES. If your trade path crosses an inverted FVG, reduce conviction.
